task.prompt: Write a function `redact_tokens(text)` that redacts tokens from text.

tests.code: assert redact_tokens('first sk-12345678 second sk-ABCDEFGH') == 'first [REDACTED] second [REDACTED]'
assert redact_tokens('nothing secret here') == 'nothing secret here'
assert redact_tokens('prefixsk-abc12345 is not separated') == 'prefixsk-abc12345 is not separated'

gold_spec.goal: Replace secret-looking API tokens in text with a fixed placeholder.

inputs.name: text
inputs.description: input text that may contain API tokens

outputs.name: redacted
outputs.description: text with matching tokens replaced by [REDACTED]

postconditions: A token starts at the beginning of the string or after whitespace.
A token starts with the literal prefix 'sk-'.
After 'sk-', a token has at least eight ASCII letters, digits, or hyphens.
Replace the whole matching token with '[REDACTED]'.
Do not redact shorter sk- strings.
Do not redact sk- substrings embedded inside a larger word.
edge_cases: Redact every matching token, not just the first.
acceptance_criteria: Only separated sk- tokens with at least eight valid trailing characters are redacted.

known_weakness.description: The raw prompt does not define token syntax, minimum length, word boundaries, or whether to redact all matches.
